mechanism_narrative: Rising CPIAUCSL levels directly increase inflation expectations, prompting equity investors to demand higher risk premiums and discount future corporate earnings at elevated rates, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 over 3-year rolling windows, or positive 20-day returns occur in >65% of high-inflation observations.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in UNRATE exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent trading week.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are positive in >55% of triggered instances, or the signal shows no statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in RSAFS and HOUST indicates robust consumer spending and housing market strength, which sustains corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative 60-day returns in >60% of cases, or interaction term loses predictive power when added to baseline regression.

mechanism_narrative: Declining INDPRO levels during periods of elevated FEDFUNDS indicate supply-side contraction under tight financial conditions, forcing earnings downgrades and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses to positive during tightening periods, or IC drops below -0.02 in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Higher FEDFUNDS rates increase borrowing costs for corporations and reduce the present value of future cash flows, mechanically suppressing equity valuations and generating negative short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no negative correlation with rate levels, or Sharpe ratio of long-short portfolio based on this signal remains below 0.2.

mechanism_narrative: Rising PAYEMS combined with falling UNRATE confirms broad-based employment growth, boosting consumer income and corporate profit expectations, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Combined labor strength signals produce negative 60-day returns in >58% of observations, or interaction effect is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly GDPC1 growth rate falling below 1.5% signals weakening aggregate demand, prompting equity market repricing toward lower earnings multiples and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Market returns remain positive in >60% of low-growth quarters, or the threshold fails to separate return distributions significantly.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp declines in INDPRO during disinflationary periods reflect demand-pull weakness rather than supply shocks, leading to flight-to-quality flows and negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Signal yields positive 5-day returns in >55% of triggered cases, or predictive accuracy vanishes when CPIAUCSL is rising.
